upper_intermediate.title: Quantifiers: All, Most, Both, Either, Neither, Any, No, None
upper_intermediate.content:
📌 ALL – Toàn bộ
all + noun (general): All plants need water.
all (of) the/my/etc. + noun (specific): All (of) the plants in the garden were burned.
all of + object pronoun: All of them were at the event.
Mid position (before main verb / after be / after 1st auxiliary):
We all went. / They were all happy. / We can all be there.
⚠️ all + time (no article/preposition): I studied all day and all night. / We'll be here all week.
📌 MOST – Đa số (hơn 50%)
most + noun (general): Most people enjoy music.
most of the/my/etc. + noun (specific): Most of the students passed.
most of + pronoun: Most of them agreed.
⚠️ Không dùng the ngay sau most khi nói chung: Most ~~the~~ people like music.
📌 BOTH / EITHER / NEITHER – Nói về HAI thứ/người
TừNghĩaCấu trúcVí dụ
bothcả hai (khẳng định)both + plural noun
both (of) the + noun
both of + pronoun
both … and …		Both students passed.
Both of them agreed.
Both Jack and Sue came.
eithermột trong hai (bất kỳ)either + singular noun
either of + the/pronoun
either … or …		Either day is fine.
Either of them will do.
Either Tom or Sue can help.
neitherkhông cái nào (phủ định)neither + singular noun
neither of + the/pronoun
neither … nor …		Neither candidate is good.
Neither of them came.
Neither Tom nor Sue knew.

⚠️ neither là từ phủ định → dùng với động từ khẳng định: Neither of them came. (KHÔNG dùng didn't come)
⚠️ either trong câu phủ định = neither: I didn't like either of them. = I liked neither of them.
📌 ANY / NO / NONE
TừDùng khi nàoVí dụ
no + nouncâu khẳng định (= not any)I have no friends. (= I don't have any friends.)
any + nouncâu phủ định / câu hỏi
hoặc = 'bất kỳ' (khẳng định)		I don't have any time.
You can come any day.
none (pronoun)thay thế cho noun (không đi kèm noun)
none of the/my/them		'Is there any milk?' 'No, there isn't any.'
None of my students is/are from France.
Most of the delegates agreed with the proposal, but none of them signed immediately.most of the (specific group) + none of them (pronoun)
Neither candidate impressed the panel – both gave unconvincing answers.neither (phủ định cả hai) + both (khẳng định cả hai)
You can use either entrance; there's no difference between them.either (bất kỳ một trong hai) + no (= not any)

exercises.q: ___ students find grammar easy. It takes time and practice.
exercises.exp: Nói chung về học sinh → most + noun (không dùng 'of' trực tiếp nếu không có 'the/my...')

exercises.q: I've read ___ the books on that shelf. They were all excellent.
exercises.exp: Nhóm cụ thể (the books) → all of + the + noun

exercises.q: ___ the candidates impressed the committee. They were all very weak.
exercises.exp: Hơn 2 ứng viên → none of (không phải neither của 2 người)

exercises.q: She invited ___ of us to her party, which was really kind of her.
exercises.exp: all of + us (object pronoun) → nhóm nhiều người

exercises.q: I don't mind which restaurant we go to. ___ option sounds good to me.
exercises.exp: Hai lựa chọn, chấp nhận một trong hai → either (= one or the other, doesn't matter)

exercises.q: ___ Jack nor his brother was at the meeting yesterday.
exercises.exp: Phủ định cả hai → neither … nor

exercises.q: We worked ___ day trying to finish the project before the deadline.
exercises.exp: all day (không dùng article/preposition) → cả ngày

exercises.q: There's ___ milk left in the fridge. Could you buy some?
exercises.exp: no + noun trong câu khẳng định (= not any)

exercises.q: 'Is there any bread?' 'No, there isn't ___.'
exercises.exp: any dùng như pronoun trong câu trả lời phủ định (không theo sau noun)

exercises.q: ___ of the two applicants had the right qualifications, so we re-advertised.
exercises.exp: Phủ định cả hai trong số HAI người → neither of

exercises.q: ___ people enjoy being criticised, even when it's constructive.
exercises.exp: Đa số → most + noun (general, không dùng 'the')

exercises.q: I tried ___ restaurants on that street. ___ of them was particularly good.
exercises.exp: both (hai nhà hàng) + neither of them (phủ định cả hai)

exercises.q: You can call me ___ day this week – I'm free the whole time.
exercises.exp: any = bất kỳ ngày nào (không quan trọng ngày nào) trong câu khẳng định

exercises.q: ___ of the students has/have submitted their essay yet. The deadline is tomorrow.
exercises.exp: Hơn 2 học sinh → none of + plural noun (singular or plural verb)

exercises.q: They were ___ exhausted after the hike – you could see it on their faces.
exercises.exp: both ở mid position (sau be/auxiliary) → both exhausted (hai người)

exercises.q: ___ the food at the party was home-made. It tasted wonderful.
exercises.exp: Toàn bộ đồ ăn cụ thể, nhưng không dùng 'of' trước 'the' khi có thể bỏ → All the food

exercises.q: I searched everywhere but could find ___ information about the event.
exercises.exp: no + uncountable noun trong câu khẳng định

exercises.q: ___ side of the argument has merit, so it's a genuinely difficult decision.
exercises.exp: either + singular noun = mỗi bên trong hai bên đều có giá trị

exercises.q: The twins ___ speak three languages. It runs in the family.
exercises.exp: both ở mid position (trước main verb) → They both speak

exercises.q: ___ of my colleagues could help – they were all in meetings all afternoon.
exercises.exp: Hơn 2 đồng nghiệp → none of (không phải neither)

exercises.q: I've contacted ___ (all) of my clients this week. They ___ (all) responded positively.
exercises.exp: all of + my clients (specific) / they all (mid position sau subject)

exercises.q: ___ (most) the applicants were qualified, but ___ (none) them got the job.
exercises.exp: most of the (specific group) / none of them (pronoun)

exercises.q: I don't like ___ (either) of those colours. ___ (neither) of them suits the room.
exercises.exp: either trong câu phủ định = neither; hoặc dùng neither trực tiếp

exercises.q: She spent ___ (all) morning preparing for the interview.
exercises.exp: all morning – không dùng article hay preposition với all + time expression

exercises.q: There's ___ (no) point arguing about it now. The decision has been made.
exercises.exp: no + noun trong câu khẳng định

exercises.q: ___ (both) the manager and the assistant agreed the plan was too risky.
exercises.exp: both … and … để kết nối hai chủ thể

exercises.q: We looked at three flats but liked ___ (none) of them.
exercises.exp: none of them (hơn 2) → pronoun, không đi kèm noun

exercises.q: You can sit on ___ (either) side of the table – it really doesn't matter.
exercises.exp: either + singular noun = một trong hai phía đều được

exercises.q: ___ (most) people underestimate how long the journey actually takes.
exercises.exp: Nói chung về people → most + noun (no 'of', no 'the')

exercises.q: The new system has ___ (no) real advantages over the old one, in my opinion.
exercises.exp: no + noun = not any → câu khẳng định

exercises.q: ___ (neither) team played well – it was a deeply disappointing final.
exercises.exp: neither + singular noun = not either of the two teams

exercises.q: I'll take ___ (any) help I can get at this point – I'm completely stuck.
exercises.exp: any = bất kỳ (it doesn't matter which) trong câu khẳng định

exercises.q: ___ (all) the evidence points to the same conclusion, which is reassuring.
exercises.exp: all + the + noun (specific evidence) = toàn bộ bằng chứng cụ thể

exercises.q: We were ___ (all) surprised by the announcement – nobody had seen it coming.
exercises.exp: we all (mid position sau subject pronoun) = tất cả chúng tôi

exercises.q: ___ (either) the red or the blue version would work well in that space.
exercises.exp: either … or … = một trong hai đều được

exercises.q: ___ (none) of the proposals we received met our minimum requirements.
exercises.exp: none of + noun phrase (hơn 2 đề xuất) → pronoun, không đi kèm noun trực tiếp

exercises.q: They were ___ (both) very tired after the long journey, but neither complained.
exercises.exp: both ở mid position (sau subject pronoun) → they both were / were both

exercises.q: ___ (most) of the feedback we received was positive, which was encouraging.
exercises.exp: most of + the + noun (specific feedback already mentioned) = đa số phần lớn

exercises.q: There's ___ (no) guarantee this will work, but it's worth trying.
exercises.exp: no + noun (guarantee) trong câu khẳng định = there isn't any guarantee

exercises.q: ___ (any) help you can offer would be greatly appreciated right now.
exercises.exp: any = bất kỳ (không quan trọng là loại giúp đỡ nào) trong câu khẳng định

reorder.words: most of the students in the class passed the final exam
neither of the two candidates was particularly impressive at the interview
we all stayed up all night to finish the report
both the manager and the assistant agreed the plan was risky
there is no point worrying about things you cannot control
none of my colleagues could attend the training session that day
you can call me any day this week I am usually free
all of them were exhausted after the long hike in the mountains
neither the food nor the service was particularly impressive at the restaurant
I searched everywhere but found no information about the missing person
most people find it difficult to speak in public without preparation
both doors lead to the same corridor so it does not matter which you use
I tried both restaurants but neither of them was worth the price
she spent all morning preparing for the presentation and it paid off
all the information we need is available on the official website
none of the proposals met the minimum requirements set by the committee
either route will get you there in about the same amount of time
the twins both speak three languages fluently which runs in the family
most of the feedback we received was genuinely positive and encouraging
there was no evidence linking the suspect to the scene of the crime

passage.text: The school recently held its annual science fair, and the results were remarkable. {{1}} of the students had worked incredibly hard, and you could see their dedication in {{2}} single project on display. {{3}} the entries were of a high standard, though a few stood out in particular. Two projects in the final were so close in quality that the judges found it difficult to choose: {{4}} project demonstrated real originality. In the end, {{5}} projects were awarded first place – an unprecedented decision. There was {{6}} clear winner in the traditional sense. The teachers were {{7}} proud of the outcome. {{8}} the parents {{9}} attending seemed genuinely impressed. {{10}} of the participants will go on to compete at the national level next spring.

City council elections often produce complicated results. In last week's vote, {{1}} of the three main parties won an outright majority. {{2}} the Progressive Party and the Reform Alliance performed better than expected, but {{3}} of them secured enough seats to govern alone. {{4}} smaller parties managed to win any seats at all, which was a blow for political diversity. The two leading parties are now in talks, but {{5}} side seems willing to compromise on its key policies. {{6}} the councillors elected are new to politics, which could make coalition-building difficult. {{7}} of the outgoing councillors retained their seats, which represents a significant shift in the political landscape. Political analysts say {{8}} major reform is likely in the short term. For now, there is {{9}} certainty about who will lead the city. {{10}} options on the table carry significant risks.

Planning a long-haul trip requires careful thought. {{1}} travellers underestimate how tiring the journey can be, especially across multiple time zones. {{2}} the destinations I am considering – Japan and New Zealand – are extraordinary, but {{3}} of them is particularly cheap or easy to reach. I have been researching {{4}} week and have found {{5}} definitive answer about the best time to visit. Friends who have been to {{6}} countries say {{7}} of them is a disappointment. I could visit {{8}} country on a shorter trip, but {{9}} option would feel rushed. Ideally, I would visit {{10}} – taking several weeks and doing justice to {{11}} destination properly.

The merger between the two tech giants has been the subject of intense debate. {{1}} sides claim the deal will benefit consumers, though economists are divided. {{2}} of the companies has a particularly clean track record when it comes to data privacy, which makes regulators nervous. Critics argue there will be {{3}} real competition left in the market if the merger goes ahead. {{4}} of the three major regulatory bodies involved has approved the deal yet. The companies say {{5}} alternative approach would achieve the same efficiencies. {{6}} experts agree that the situation is unprecedented. {{7}} the legal challenges ahead will be resolved quickly. There is simply {{8}} shortcut through the process. For now, {{9}} sides must wait, and {{10}} involved hopes for a swift resolution.

Learning a new language is a challenge that {{1}} adult learners face differently. {{2}} methods work for everyone – {{3}} person learns in their own way and at their own pace. Some people find that {{4}} grammar rules matter and that exposure is everything; others feel {{5}} progress is possible without systematic study. Most language schools offer two main programmes: intensive and standard. {{6}} option has its advantages. {{7}} programmes are popular, and {{8}} of them produces strong results when students put in the effort. There is {{9}} magic shortcut, however. {{10}} successful language learners will tell you the same thing: consistency, patience, and a willingness to make mistakes are {{11}} essential.

writing_prompts.vi: So sánh hai thành phố, quốc gia, hoặc nơi bạn đã từng sống hoặc thăm – hãy dùng both, either, neither, all, none để so sánh điểm giống và khác nhau về văn hóa, con người, môi trường sống và cơ sở hạ tầng
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: Quantifiers: both, either, neither, all, most, none in comparative context
writing_prompts.example: Having lived in both Hanoi and Ho Chi Minh City, I feel well placed to compare them. Both cities are vibrant and full of energy, and neither of them ever really sleeps. Most of the people I met in both places were warm and welcoming, though the pace of life differs considerably. Hanoi has a certain elegance – most of its old quarter streets are narrow and shaded, and neither the traffic nor the noise reaches quite the same intensity as in the south. Ho Chi Minh City, on the other hand, has a more commercial feel. Both cities have world-class street food, and I would argue that neither is clearly superior in that regard. All the experiences I had in both places were memorable for different reasons. If I had to choose, either city would make an excellent home, but I think most long-term expats tend to gravitate towards the south for its more international atmosphere. Ultimately, there is no single right answer – it depends entirely on what you value.

writing_prompts.vi: Viết về một quyết định khó khăn bạn đã phải đưa ra khi phải lựa chọn giữa hai lựa chọn – dùng either/neither/both/none để diễn đạt sự phân vân và kết quả cuối cùng
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: Quantifiers: either, neither, both, none with decision-making context
writing_prompts.example: One of the hardest decisions I have ever had to make was choosing between two very different career paths. I had been offered a place on a postgraduate programme at a prestigious university abroad, and at the same time, I had received a job offer from a company I had long admired. Neither option was easy to turn down, and both came with significant advantages. Most of my friends and family had strong opinions, but none of the advice I received made the decision any clearer. Either choice would have taken my life in a completely different direction. On one hand, further study would open academic doors; on the other, the job would provide financial stability and real-world experience. Both possibilities excited me, but neither felt entirely right for exactly the same reasons. In the end, I chose the job. All things considered, I felt the timing was better, and I did not want to take on more debt. I have never regretted it, though I sometimes wonder what the other path might have looked like.

writing_prompts.vi: Hãy viết về thói quen, sở thích hoặc cuộc sống của hầu hết người dân ở đất nước hoặc vùng miền bạn quen thuộc – dùng most, all, no, none, any để mô tả các đặc điểm chung
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: Quantifiers: most, all, no, none, any in descriptive/cultural context
writing_prompts.example: Life in the Mekong Delta region of Vietnam has a rhythm that most outsiders find immediately appealing. Most families here have lived on or near the water for generations, and all the major festivals are celebrated with extraordinary enthusiasm. Food is central to everything – most social gatherings, however informal, involve an enormous spread of dishes, and no visitor leaves a home without being offered something to eat. There is no shortage of fresh produce, and most of the ingredients in local dishes are grown within just a few kilometres of the table. None of the meals I have eaten in the region could be described as forgettable. Most evenings, people gather along the riverbanks, and any traveller willing to sit and watch the sunset will find themselves warmly welcomed into a conversation. Most of the younger generation are moving to the cities, which is changing the social fabric of the communities. All the same, the traditions are remarkably resilient, and none of the locals I have spoken to seem ready to abandon them.

writing_prompts.vi: Viết về một vấn đề xã hội hoặc môi trường quan trọng – hãy dùng all, most, none, no, both, neither để phân tích nguyên nhân, tác động và các giải pháp được đề xuất
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: Quantifiers in analytical/argumentative writing: all, most, both, neither, none, no
writing_prompts.example: Air pollution in rapidly developing cities is a problem that affects all residents, regardless of income or social status. Most scientists agree that the primary causes are vehicle emissions and industrial activity, and both factors contribute roughly equally in most urban environments. All the evidence gathered over the past two decades points to a clear link between poor air quality and serious respiratory conditions. Neither the government nor the private sector has taken sufficiently bold action, and most of the measures introduced so far have been too limited in scope to make a meaningful difference. No single policy will solve the problem on its own. All stakeholders – including individuals, businesses, and governments – must take responsibility. Most of the cities that have made progress have done so by combining public transport investment with strict emissions regulations. None of the solutions are cheap or politically easy, and neither side of the debate tends to acknowledge the full complexity of the issue. All the same, the cost of inaction is far higher than the cost of reform, and most economists agree that the longer we wait, the more expensive the solution becomes.

writing_prompts.vi: Mô tả trải nghiệm xem/đọc/nghe hai tác phẩm nghệ thuật, phim, sách hoặc album nhạc – dùng both, either, neither, all, most, none để so sánh chúng và đưa ra đánh giá tổng thể
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: Quantifiers in cultural comparison and evaluation
writing_prompts.example: I recently watched both films in a celebrated director's latest double release, and I found it almost impossible to choose between them. Both films are visually stunning, and neither feels like filler or a lesser companion piece. All the performances are exceptional – most of the lead actors have clearly done enormous research into their roles. Most of the reviews I had read warned that neither film was easy to watch, and both deal with genuinely difficult subject matter. However, neither feels gratuitously dark; all the difficult scenes serve the story. If I had to recommend one to a casual viewer, I would say either would work as a standalone experience, though neither makes complete sense without the context the other provides. Most audiences, I suspect, will find the second more immediately satisfying, as most of the major threads are resolved there. None of the plot twists felt contrived or manipulative. All in all, both films are among the finest I have seen in years, and neither the critics nor the general audience seem to disagree on that point.

upper_intermediate.title: Whatever, Whenever, Wherever, Whoever, However
upper_intermediate.content:
📌 -ever words – Nghĩa và cách dùng
Khi thêm -ever vào sau các từ để hỏi (what, when, where, who, how), ta tạo ra từ mới với nghĩa "bất kỳ... nào cũng được" – tức là sự lựa chọn không bị giới hạn hoặc không quan trọng.

TừNghĩaVí dụ
whatever'bất cứ thứ gì' / 'dù là gì đi nữa'
(= anything, no matter what)		Do whatever you like.
I am proud of you, whatever happens.
whenever'bất cứ khi nào' / 'mỗi khi'
(= any time, every time)		Call me whenever you need help.
I feel nervous whenever I speak in public.
wherever'bất cứ nơi nào' / 'dù ở đâu'
(= anywhere, no matter where)		Sit wherever you like.
She feels at home wherever she is.
whoever'bất cứ ai' / 'người nào... cũng'
(= anyone who, no matter who)		Invite whoever you want.
Whoever finishes first wins a prize.
however'theo bất kỳ cách nào' / 'dù thế nào'
(= in any way, no matter how)		Customise it however you want.
However hard he tries, he never gives up.
whichever'bất cứ cái nào' (trong số có hạn)
(= any one from a limited set)		Take whichever seat you prefer.
Whichever route you take, allow extra time.
📌 Hai cách dùng chính
1. Làm mệnh đề phụ (adverbial / noun clause):
You can eat whatever you like. (noun clause – tân ngữ)
She goes wherever the job takes her. (adverbial clause – nơi chốn)
Whoever calls, tell them I'm busy. (adverbial – điều kiện nhượng bộ)

2. Diễn đạt sự nhượng bộ (concession) – 'dù... thế nào':
Whatever you decide, I'll support you. (= No matter what you decide)
However tired I am, I always exercise. (= No matter how tired)
Wherever we go, we always have fun. (= No matter where)

⚠️ however + adj/adv (không dùng 'no matter how + adj'): However difficult it is, don't give up.
⚠️ Không nhầm với câu hỏi: What did you choose? (KHÔNG phải Whatever did you choose?)
You can decorate the apartment however you want – it's yours now.however = in any way you want
Whoever is responsible for this mess needs to clean it up immediately.whoever = the person who (no matter who it is)
Whenever I hear that song, I think of our summer holiday years ago.whenever = every time (repeated action/feeling)

exercises.q: You can borrow ___ book you like from my shelf. I don't mind.
exercises.exp: whatever = any thing / bất kỳ quyển sách nào

exercises.q: Call me ___ you need help. I'm always available.
exercises.exp: whenever = any time / bất cứ khi nào

exercises.q: ___ is responsible for this damage will have to pay for the repairs.
exercises.exp: whoever = the person who / bất cứ ai đã gây ra thiệt hại

exercises.q: Technology now allows many people to work from ___ they want.
exercises.exp: wherever = any place / bất cứ nơi nào

exercises.q: ___ hard the task is, we need to find a solution before Friday.
exercises.exp: however + adj = no matter how + adj

exercises.q: Take ___ seat you prefer – they're all equally comfortable.
exercises.exp: whichever = any one from a limited set of options (seats)

exercises.q: ___ you decide, make sure it's something you won't regret.
exercises.exp: whatever = no matter what (concession / nhượng bộ)

exercises.q: He tends to get nervous ___ he has to speak in front of a large audience.
exercises.exp: whenever = every time (hành động lặp lại)

exercises.q: She feels completely at home ___ she travels in the world.
exercises.exp: wherever = in any place / dù ở đâu trên thế giới

exercises.q: Please let ___ arrives first know that the meeting has been moved.
exercises.exp: whoever = the person who / bất cứ ai đến trước

exercises.q: ___ much it costs, I'm determined to see the Northern Lights before I'm forty.
exercises.exp: however much = no matter how much (+ amount)

exercises.q: You can customise the interface ___ suits you – all options are available.
exercises.exp: whatever = anything that / bất cứ cách nào phù hợp (noun clause)

exercises.q: I seem to bump into my old teacher ___ I go in this city.
exercises.exp: wherever = in every place I go / ở bất cứ đâu

exercises.q: ___ wins the election will face enormous economic challenges.
exercises.exp: whoever = the person who wins (subject của mệnh đề)

exercises.q: The door is always open. Come round ___ you feel like it.
exercises.exp: whenever = any time you want to

exercises.q: ___ long it takes, I intend to master this language completely.
exercises.exp: however long = no matter how long (+ adjective/adverb)

exercises.q: Invite ___ you want to the dinner party. The more the merrier.
exercises.exp: whoever = anyone (object của invite)

exercises.q: I'll support your decision ___ you choose to do.
exercises.exp: whatever you choose = no matter what you choose

exercises.q: ___ the outcome of the match, the players can be proud of their performance.
exercises.exp: whatever = no matter what the outcome is (concession)

exercises.q: She manages to stay calm ___ stressed or overwhelmed she feels.
exercises.exp: however + adj = no matter how stressed/overwhelmed

exercises.q: You can paint the walls ___ colour you like – this is your home now.
exercises.exp: whatever colour = any colour / bất kỳ màu nào

exercises.q: ___ I try to explain it, she just doesn't seem to understand.
exercises.exp: however (hard/carefully) = no matter how I explain

exercises.q: ___ gets to the finish line first will be declared the champion.
exercises.exp: whoever = the person who (chủ ngữ của mệnh đề)

exercises.q: I feel anxious ___ I have to make an important phone call.
exercises.exp: whenever = every time / mỗi khi

exercises.q: He follows the same routine ___ he goes in the world.
exercises.exp: wherever = in any place / dù ở đâu

exercises.q: Take ___ path looks easier – they both lead to the summit eventually.
exercises.exp: whichever = any one of a limited number of options (paths)

exercises.q: ___ the weather is like tomorrow, the outdoor event will go ahead.
exercises.exp: whatever = no matter what the weather is (concession)

exercises.q: ___ carefully you read the instructions, some mistakes are inevitable.
exercises.exp: however + adverb = no matter how carefully

exercises.q: Feel free to drop in ___ you're passing – the kettle is always on.
exercises.exp: whenever = any time you happen to pass

exercises.q: ___ sent this letter clearly knows a lot of personal details about us.
exercises.exp: whoever = the unknown person who (subject)

exercises.q: You can dress ___ you like to the event – there is no dress code.
exercises.exp: however = in any way / theo bất kỳ cách nào

exercises.q: She seems to make friends ___ she goes, which is a wonderful quality.
exercises.exp: wherever = in every place she goes

exercises.q: Do ___ it takes to get the project finished on time.
exercises.exp: whatever = anything / bất cứ điều gì cần làm

exercises.q: ___ you may think of him personally, you have to admit he's talented.
exercises.exp: whatever = no matter what / dù bạn nghĩ gì về anh ấy

exercises.q: ___ designed this building clearly had a very unusual sense of aesthetics.
exercises.exp: whoever = the person who (subject của clause)

exercises.q: ___ (whichever) of the two proposals is selected, the team will need extra funding.
exercises.exp: whichever = any one from a limited set (two proposals) trong mệnh đề nhượng bộ

exercises.q: The company will reimburse ___ (whatever) expenses you incur on the business trip.
exercises.exp: whatever expenses = any expenses / bất kỳ chi phí nào (noun clause modifier)

exercises.q: ___ (Whenever) she feels overwhelmed, she goes for a long walk to clear her head.
exercises.exp: whenever = every time / each time she feels this way (repeated action)

exercises.q: The final decision rests with ___ (whoever) the board appoints as the new chairperson.
exercises.exp: whoever the board appoints = the person whom the board appoints (object clause)

exercises.q: ___ (However) you look at it, the data clearly supports the original hypothesis.
exercises.exp: however you look at it = no matter how / from whatever angle you examine it

reorder.words: you can sit wherever you like there are plenty of seats available
whoever arrives first should put the coffee on for the rest of us
however hard she tries she never seems to be able to please everyone
call me whenever you need help I am always here for you
whatever you decide I will support you one hundred percent
take whichever route looks clearest on the map and add extra time
she feels completely at home wherever she travels in the world
however long it takes we are determined to find a solution together
invite whoever you want to the party the more the merrier
whatever the outcome we should be proud of everything we have achieved
he gets nervous whenever he has to speak in front of a large crowd
whoever is responsible for this needs to come forward immediately
however much it costs I am going to buy those concert tickets
you can decorate the room however you want it is entirely your choice
wherever the company sends her she always adapts quickly and performs well
whatever happens tomorrow we are fully prepared for every possible scenario
whenever I hear that song I immediately think of my school days
choose whichever option seems most practical given your current situation
whoever wrote this report clearly spent a great deal of time on it
however tired I am I always try to read a few pages before sleeping

passage.text: My grandmother has always lived by a simple philosophy: enjoy life {{1}} it takes you. {{2}} she goes, whether it is the local market or a foreign country, she brings the same warmth and curiosity. She says that {{3}} comes her way – good or bad – is an opportunity to learn. {{4}} I visit her, she has a new story to tell. {{5}} difficult life gets, she insists on finding something to laugh about. She will talk to {{6}} is willing to listen, and she has a gift for making {{7}} she meets feel genuinely valued. I asked her once how she stays so positive. She said, '{{8}} you go through in life, it is how you respond that defines you. And {{9}} long the road is, just keep walking.' {{10}} gives that kind of advice needs no qualifications – just a long life and a generous spirit.

The charity operates on one simple rule: help {{1}} needs it most, {{2}} they are. {{3}} a crisis hits – a flood, a fire, a famine – the team mobilises. They work with {{4}} local organisations are best placed to distribute aid, and they accept donations {{5}} they come from. Their founder has said that {{6}} the scale of the disaster, no community should be left to cope alone. Volunteers are encouraged to get involved {{7}} they can, in {{8}} way they are most useful. {{9}} donates to the charity, whether it is a child giving pocket money or a corporation giving millions, the impact is real. The team believes that {{10}} hard the challenge, people working together will always find a way through.

Online learning has transformed education in ways that were unimaginable a generation ago. Students can now study {{1}} they want – at home, in a café, or on a train. They can begin a lesson {{2}} is convenient for them, pausing and rewinding {{3}} they need to revisit a concept. {{4}} designs a good online course understands that learners have different needs. The best platforms allow students to choose {{5}} level of support they require and progress at {{6}} pace suits them best. {{7}} slow a learner may be, the content adapts to their needs. {{8}} the subject – mathematics, history, music, coding – there is likely a high-quality course available online. Instructors can teach from {{9}} in the world, and {{10}} wants to build a skill no longer needs to be in the same room as their teacher. It is, by any measure, a revolution.

Great leadership is not about having all the answers – it is about knowing how to respond {{1}} challenges arise. A true leader remains calm {{2}} difficult or pressured the situation becomes. They listen to {{3}} has something valuable to contribute, regardless of rank or experience. They make decisions {{4}} the evidence points, even if the direction is unpopular. They are accessible {{5}} their team needs guidance and are consistent {{6}} they are – in the boardroom or in the corridor. {{7}} the outcome of a decision, a good leader takes responsibility. They motivate {{8}} works for them to give their best, and they adapt their approach to suit {{9}} individual they are working with. {{10}} eventually achieves greatness in leadership does so not by seeking power, but by serving others with dedication and humility.

My best friend and I have been travelling together for years, and our rule is simple: go {{1}} looks interesting, eat {{2}} smells good, and say yes to {{3}} the locals recommend. {{4}} we arrive somewhere new, we make a point of wandering without a plan. {{5}} we end up – a tiny mountain village or a chaotic city market – we always find something worth remembering. We talk to {{6}} is willing to share their story, and we've met extraordinary people that way. {{7}} tired or jet-lagged we feel, we push ourselves to explore on the first day. {{8}} mode of transport is available, we take it – bus, boat, bicycle, or foot. {{9}} we go back to a place we've visited before, it always feels different. {{10}} joins us on a trip quickly learns that there is only one rule: leave your comfort zone at the airport.

writing_prompts.vi: Hãy mô tả một người bạn biết (hoặc một nhân vật nổi tiếng) có khả năng thích nghi và linh hoạt đáng kinh ngạc – họ làm tốt ở bất cứ đâu, bất cứ khi nào, với bất cứ ai – dùng các từ -ever để diễn đạt sự linh hoạt đó
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: whatever, whenever, wherever, whoever, however in descriptive writing
writing_prompts.example: My aunt is the most adaptable person I have ever met. Wherever life has taken her – and it has taken her to some remarkable places – she has always found a way to thrive. She spent years working as a volunteer nurse, going wherever the need was greatest, from remote mountain communities to overcrowded urban hospitals. Whenever a crisis arose, she was always one of the first to step forward. Whatever the conditions, whether there was electricity, clean water, or adequate equipment, she found a way to provide care. Whoever she worked with – experienced doctors or untrained local volunteers – she treated them with equal respect and made them feel capable. However exhausted she became, she would push through because she genuinely believed the work mattered. She once told me that the secret to happiness was simple: go wherever you are needed, do whatever you can with what you have, and care about whoever is in front of you. I have tried to live by those words ever since.

writing_prompts.vi: Viết về sự tự do trong cuộc sống hiện đại – khả năng lựa chọn nơi làm việc, khi nào làm việc và cách sống – hãy dùng wherever, whenever, whatever, however để thể hiện sự phong phú của các lựa chọn ngày nay so với thế hệ trước
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: wherever, whenever, whatever, however expressing freedom and unlimited choice
writing_prompts.example: One of the most significant shifts of the past two decades is the degree to which individuals can now design their lives on their own terms. People can work from wherever they choose – a flat in Lisbon, a co-working space in Tokyo, or a beach café in Vietnam – as long as they have a reliable internet connection. They can log on whenever it suits them, structure their days however they prefer, and collaborate with whoever shares their goals, regardless of geography. For my parents' generation, these possibilities were almost unimaginable. Wherever you were born, you were largely expected to stay. Whatever career you chose, you generally did it in one place, in one way, for one employer. Today, the rules have changed. Whatever your passion, there is likely a market for it somewhere online. However unconventional your skills, you can find an audience. The challenge, of course, is making wise choices when the options are essentially unlimited. Having the freedom to go wherever and do whatever can, paradoxically, make it harder to commit to anything at all.

writing_prompts.vi: Viết về một triết lý sống hoặc nguyên tắc cá nhân mà bạn trân trọng – hãy dùng các từ -ever để giải thích cách bạn áp dụng nguyên tắc đó trong mọi tình huống của cuộc sống
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: whatever, whenever, wherever, whoever, however expressing personal values and principles
writing_prompts.example: The principle I try to live by is deceptively simple: be honest, whatever the consequences. I have not always found it easy, but I have found that a commitment to honesty shapes everything else. Wherever I find myself – in a job interview, a difficult conversation, or a moment of personal failure – I try to tell the truth as clearly and kindly as I can. Whenever I am tempted to take the easier path of a small deception, I remind myself of how much I dislike being misled by others. However people react to honesty in the short term, I believe the long-term effect on relationships and self-respect is always worth it. Whatever the outcome of any particular conversation, I would rather be known as someone who speaks truthfully than someone who says whatever seems most convenient. Whoever I am with – friends, colleagues, or strangers – I try to give them the same level of candour I would want for myself. However long it has taken me to develop this habit, I consider it one of the most important things I have worked on.

writing_prompts.vi: Kể một câu chuyện ngắn về một chuyến phiêu lưu hoặc hành trình bất ngờ – trong đó mọi quyết định đều mang tính ngẫu hứng (đi đâu, làm gì, gặp ai) – dùng các từ -ever để diễn đạt sự tự phát và không có kế hoạch
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: wherever, whenever, whatever, whoever, however in narrative writing
writing_prompts.example: The summer I turned twenty-five, my friend and I decided to travel with no plan whatsoever. We went wherever the next bus or train happened to be going. We ate whatever was being cooked at the nearest market stall and slept wherever we could find a spare room. We talked to whoever seemed interesting and stayed for however long felt right – sometimes a single night, sometimes a whole week. Whenever we grew restless, we moved on. Whenever something caught our eye – a festival, a mountain path, a crumbling church – we stopped. One evening, we ended up at a village wedding, having been invited in by whoever was standing at the gate. We spoke almost no common language with our hosts, but we danced however the music moved us and laughed however the mood took us. Whatever that trip lacked in comfort, it more than made up for in freedom. I returned home with no photos, no souvenirs, and no clear itinerary to report – just the absolute certainty that going wherever life points is sometimes the best plan of all.

writing_prompts.vi: Viết về tầm quan trọng của sự kiên trì – hãy dùng 'however + adj/adv' để nói về việc không bỏ cuộc dù hoàn cảnh khó khăn thế nào, và 'whatever/whenever/wherever' để mô tả các tình huống thử thách
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: however + adj/adv (concession); whatever/whenever/wherever in challenging contexts
hint_words: wherever you are in your journey
writing_prompts.example: One of the most valuable lessons I have learned is that persistence, applied consistently, will eventually overcome almost any obstacle. However difficult the challenge, there is almost always a way forward if you are willing to keep looking. I have been through periods where whatever I tried seemed to fail, and whenever I felt close to a breakthrough, something would set me back. However many times I stumbled, I tried to get up and begin again. The temptation to quit is strongest, I find, when you are closest to succeeding – however counterintuitive that may sound. Wherever you are in your own journey, however small the progress you are making, it is worth remembering that movement in the right direction, however slow, is still movement. Whatever obstacles appear – and they will – treat them as information rather than defeat. However discouraging the feedback or the results, ask what you can learn from them. Whenever you feel like stopping, take a rest by all means, but do not mistake exhaustion for failure. The people who ultimately succeed are rarely the most talented. They are, almost always, the ones who kept going however hard it got.

upper_intermediate.title: Auxiliary Verbs: Different Uses
upper_intermediate.content:
📌 1. Đồng ý (Agreement): So / Neither / Nor
Dùng auxiliary verb với so (đồng ý câu khẳng định) và neither/nor (đồng ý câu phủ định) để tránh lặp lại động từ.

So + aux + subject → đồng ý câu khẳng định:
A: 'I love Thai food.' B: 'So do I.'
A: 'She's been to Japan.' B: 'So have I.'

Neither/Nor + aux + subject → đồng ý câu phủ định:
A: 'I'm not going to accept.' B: 'Neither am I.'
A: 'I can't concentrate.' B: 'Nor can I.'
A: 'I didn't like the food.' B: 'Neither did I.'

⚠️ Nếu câu trước có auxiliary verb → dùng cùng auxiliary đó.
⚠️ Nếu không có auxiliary → dùng do/does/did.
📌 2. Phản bác (Disagreement)
Dùng subject + auxiliary verb để phản bác.
A: 'You never help.' B: 'I do help! I cooked dinner last night!'
A: 'She's not reliable.' B: 'She is reliable! She's never late.'
⚠️ Ở Present/Past Simple không có auxiliary → dùng thêm do/does/did:
A: 'You didn't say sorry.' B: 'I did say sorry, but he didn't hear me.'
📌 3. Nhấn mạnh (Emphasis) với do/does/did
Thêm do/does/did trước main verb để nhấn mạnh sự thật (thường khi phản bác hoặc bày tỏ cảm xúc mạnh):
A: 'Sally doesn't speak Spanish.' B: 'She does speak Spanish – she lived in Madrid!'
I do understand your concern, but I can't change the decision.
He did try his best, even if the result was disappointing.
📌 4. Question Tags – Đuôi câu hỏi
Dùng để xin xác nhận hoặc kiểm tra thông tin đã nghĩ là đúng.

Câu khẳng định + negative tag? (giọng xuống → muốn xác nhận)
You're coming to the party, aren't you?
She's lived here for years, hasn't she?

Câu phủ định + positive tag?
He didn't call, did he?
They won't be late, will they?

⚠️ Trường hợp đặc biệt:
I'm right, aren't I? (không dùng 'amn't')
Let's go, shall we?
Don't do that, will you?
Everything is ready, isn't it?
'I've never been to New Zealand.' 'Neither have I, but I'd love to go.'Neither + aux (have) + subject → đồng ý câu phủ định hiện tại hoàn thành
A: 'You don't care about this.' B: 'I do care – I just show it differently.'do + main verb → nhấn mạnh sự thật trong hiện tại
The presentation went well, didn't it? Everyone seemed really engaged.Positive sentence + negative tag (didn't) → xin xác nhận

exercises.q: 'I've never tried skydiving.' '___, but I'd like to one day.'
exercises.opts: Neither have I,So have I,Neither do I,So do I
exercises.exp: Đồng ý câu phủ định (never tried) → neither + have (present perfect) + I

exercises.q: 'She works really hard.' '___, and she deserves a promotion.'
exercises.opts: So she does,So does she,Neither does she,Nor she does
exercises.exp: Đồng ý câu khẳng định (works – present simple) → so + does + she

exercises.q: 'I'm not going to the meeting tomorrow.' '___ – I have another commitment.'
exercises.opts: Neither am I,Nor I am,So am I,Neither I am
exercises.exp: Đồng ý câu phủ định (not going) → neither + am (be) + I

exercises.q: A: 'You never listen to me.' B: 'I ___ listen – I just don't always agree!'
exercises.exp: Nhấn mạnh sự thật ở hiện tại với present simple → do + listen

exercises.q: You've been to Vietnam before, ___?
exercises.exp: Câu khẳng định (have been) → negative tag: haven't you

exercises.q: 'I didn't enjoy the concert.' '___ – the sound quality was terrible.'
exercises.opts: Neither did I,So did I,Neither have I,Nor I did
exercises.exp: Đồng ý câu phủ định (didn't enjoy) → neither + did + I

exercises.q: A: 'Tom doesn't know the answer.' B: 'He ___ know – he told me this morning!'
exercises.exp: Phản bác + nhấn mạnh ở hiện tại → does + know (present simple)

exercises.q: The flight leaves at six, ___?
exercises.exp: Câu khẳng định (leaves – present simple) → negative tag: doesn't it

exercises.q: 'I can't stand horror films.' '___ – they give me nightmares.'
exercises.opts: Neither can I,So can I,Nor I can,Neither I can
exercises.exp: Đồng ý câu phủ định (can't) → neither + can (modal) + I

exercises.q: A: 'You didn't tell me about the change.' B: 'I ___ tell you – I emailed you twice!'
exercises.exp: Phản bác ở quá khứ → did + tell (past simple emphasis)

exercises.q: She hardly ever makes mistakes, ___?
exercises.exp: hardly ever = negative meaning → dùng positive tag: does she

exercises.q: 'I'm really enjoying this course.' '___ – it's been eye-opening.'
exercises.opts: So am I,So do I,Neither am I,So I am
exercises.exp: Đồng ý câu khẳng định (am enjoying) → so + am (be) + I

exercises.q: Let's take a break, ___?
exercises.exp: Let's … → tag đặc biệt: shall we?

exercises.q: 'I won't be able to make it on Friday.' '___ – I have a dentist appointment.'
exercises.opts: Neither will I,Nor I will,So won't I,Neither I will
exercises.exp: Đồng ý câu phủ định (won't) → neither + will + I

exercises.q: A: 'Maria doesn't care about the results.' B: 'She ___ care – she's been working on this for months!'
exercises.exp: Phản bác + nhấn mạnh hiện tại → does + care

exercises.q: They had never met before the conference, ___?
exercises.exp: Câu phủ định (had never) → positive tag: had they

exercises.q: 'I love a good mystery novel.' '___ – I finished one just last night.'
exercises.opts: So do I,So am I,Neither do I,So I do
exercises.exp: Đồng ý câu khẳng định (love – present simple) → so + do + I

exercises.q: I'm right about this, ___?
exercises.opts: aren't I,am I not,isn't I,amn't I
exercises.exp: I am … → tag đặc biệt: aren't I (không dùng amn't)

exercises.q: 'I haven't spoken to her in months.' '___ – we should reach out.'
exercises.opts: Neither have I,Nor I have,So have I,Neither I have
exercises.exp: Đồng ý câu phủ định (haven't) → neither + have + I

exercises.q: A: 'You don't really want to leave.' B: 'I ___ want to leave – I've been planning this for a year!'
exercises.exp: Phản bác + nhấn mạnh hiện tại → do + want

exercises.q: 'I've already booked the tickets.' '___ – I did it yesterday morning.'
exercises.exp: Đồng ý câu khẳng định (have booked – present perfect) → so + have + I

exercises.q: 'We can't come to the dinner.' '___ – we have a prior engagement.'
exercises.ans: Neither can we / Nor can we
exercises.exp: Đồng ý câu phủ định (can't) → neither/nor + can + we

exercises.q: A: 'You forgot to lock the door.' B: 'I ___ lock it – I checked twice before leaving!'
exercises.exp: Phản bác ở quá khứ → did + lock (emphasis)

exercises.q: She hadn't been told about the change, ___?
exercises.exp: Câu phủ định (hadn't been) → positive tag: had she

exercises.q: 'I'm not sure what to do.' '___ – maybe we should ask someone.'
exercises.ans: Neither am I / Nor am I
exercises.exp: Đồng ý câu phủ định (am not) → neither/nor + am + I

exercises.q: A: 'She doesn't know about the surprise party.' B: 'She ___ know! I told her by mistake yesterday!'
exercises.exp: Phản bác + nhấn mạnh → does + know (present simple)

exercises.q: 'I'd love to travel more.' '___ – I wish I had more holiday allowance.'
exercises.exp: Đồng ý câu khẳng định (would love) → so + would + I

exercises.q: Don't be late, ___?
exercises.exp: Mệnh lệnh phủ định → tag đặc biệt: will you

exercises.q: 'I didn't sleep well last night.' '___ – the noise from the street was awful.'
exercises.ans: Neither did I / Nor did I
exercises.exp: Đồng ý câu phủ định (didn't sleep) → neither/nor + did + I

exercises.q: A: 'You never appreciated what I did.' B: 'I ___ appreciate it – I told you many times!'
exercises.exp: Phản bác ở quá khứ → did + appreciate (nhấn mạnh past)

exercises.q: Everything went according to plan, ___?
exercises.exp: Câu khẳng định (went – past simple) → negative tag: didn't it

exercises.q: 'I won't be staying for dinner.' '___ – I have an early start tomorrow.'
exercises.ans: Neither will I / Nor will I
exercises.exp: Đồng ý câu phủ định (won't) → neither/nor + will + I

exercises.q: A: 'She wasn't happy about the decision.' B: 'She ___ happy – she told me she fully agreed!'
exercises.exp: Phản bác với be → was + happy (emphasis với be)

exercises.q: 'I've been really stressed lately.' '___ – it's been a difficult month for all of us.'
exercises.exp: Đồng ý câu khẳng định (have been) → so + have + I

exercises.q: Nobody called while I was out, ___?
exercises.exp: nobody = negative → positive tag: did they

exercises.q: 'I'm finding this project really stressful.' '___ – let's talk about it after the meeting.'
exercises.exp: Đồng ý câu khẳng định (am finding – present continuous) → so + am + I

exercises.q: A: 'You didn't read the brief properly.' B: 'I ___ read it – every single page!'
exercises.exp: Phản bác ở quá khứ với past simple → did + read (emphasis)

exercises.q: You should have told me earlier, ___?
exercises.exp: Câu khẳng định (should have) → negative tag: shouldn't you

exercises.q: 'I'd rather not go to the party.' '___ – I'm feeling quite tired tonight.'
exercises.ans: Neither would I / Nor would I
exercises.exp: Đồng ý câu phủ định (would rather not) → neither/nor + would + I

exercises.q: A: 'He doesn't take his studies seriously.' B: 'He ___ take them seriously – he studied until midnight!'
exercises.exp: Phản bác + nhấn mạnh hiện tại → does + take (present simple emphasis)

reorder.words: I have never been to new zealand and neither has my sister
she does care about the results she has worked on this for months
the train leaves at half past eight doesn't it
I did tell you about the change I sent you an email last week
she hardly ever complains about anything does she
I can not come to the party and neither can my husband
let us take a short break before we continue shall we
I am right about this aren't I you said the same thing yesterday
she was at the meeting yesterday wasn't she I thought I saw her
I love this kind of weather and so does my whole family
nobody told me about the cancellation did they I just found out
I did try my best even if the final result was disappointing
he had never met her before the conference had he
she won't be joining us for dinner and nor will her partner
I am enjoying this more than I expected and so is everyone else
don't forget to bring your passport will you it is very important
she does understand the situation she just needs more time to process it
I didn't enjoy the film and neither did my friends to be honest
he would love to travel more and so would most of his colleagues
everything is ready for the presentation isn't it we double checked earlier

passage.text: At the end of a long team meeting, the manager asked everyone to share one thing they had found challenging about the project. 'I found the timeline really difficult,' said Ana. '{{1}},' said Ben immediately. 'And I {{2}} find the communication between departments frustrating – though I know we {{3}} improve.' The manager nodded. 'I {{4}} understand how hard this has been. You {{5}} all worked incredibly hard, and {{6}} has your support team.' She turned to Carlos. 'You {{7}} happy with how things went, {{8}}?' Carlos smiled. 'Actually, I {{9}} – more than I expected. The launch {{10}} go smoothly in the end, {{11}}? Let's remember that.' Ana looked around the table. 'I'd love to celebrate properly.' '{{12}},' said Ben. 'Shall we book something, {{13}}?'

Two old friends, Lan and Minh, catch up after several years apart.
'I've been thinking about going back to university,' said Lan.
'{{1}},' replied Minh. 'I looked into it last year actually.'
'Really? You {{2}} mention that.'
'I {{3}} mention it – I sent you a message in January!'
'Oh, I'm sorry. I {{4}} been very good at replying lately, {{5}}?'
'No, but I understand. You've had a lot on.'
'I {{6}} appreciate you saying that. Anyway, I {{7}} really enjoy studying again, {{8}}?'
'You {{9}} – you always loved learning new things.'
'Neither of us {{10}} getting any younger, {{11}}?'
'Ha! {{12}}. But {{13}} always say it's never too late.'
'They {{14}}. And I {{15}} believe that – I just need to act on it.'

A group of students discuss an upcoming exam.
'I haven't started revising yet,' admitted Clara.
'{{1}},' said Dan. 'And I {{2}} intend to leave it this late – it just happened.'
'I {{3}} understand that,' said Emma. 'But we {{4}} start now, {{5}}?'
'We {{6}},' said Dan. 'I {{7}} find it easier if we study together, {{8}}?'
'Probably,' said Clara. 'The exam {{9}} going to be difficult, {{10}} it?'
'It {{11}}. But we {{12}} all passed harder things before, {{13}}?'
'{{14}},' said Emma. 'And the teacher {{15}} say revision guides are on the portal.'
'She {{16}} say that – I just couldn't find them.'
'I {{17}} look again. They {{18}} be there somewhere, {{19}}?'
'They {{20}}. Let's be optimistic.'

A job interview goes in an unexpected direction.
Interviewer: 'You haven't worked in this sector before, {{1}} you?'
Candidate: 'That's true, I {{2}}. But I {{3}} have relevant experience – I managed a large team in a related field for five years.'
Interviewer: 'I see. You {{4}} mention that in your cover letter.'
Candidate: 'I {{5}} mention it – it was in the third paragraph, under 'leadership experience'.'
Interviewer: 'Ah. So you {{6}}. My apologies. And you'd be comfortable relocating, {{7}}?'
Candidate: 'Absolutely. I {{8}} love the opportunity to work in a new city.'
Interviewer: 'Most candidates say that, but not many follow through.'
Candidate: 'I {{9}} mean it. I've relocated twice before for work.'
Interviewer: 'Good to hear. Well, you {{10}} done your research thoroughly, {{11}} you?'
Candidate: 'I hope {{12}}. I believe in being well-prepared.'
Interviewer: 'So {{13}} I. That's reassuring.'

Two neighbours discuss a local issue.
'Nobody told us about the road closure, {{1}} they?'
'{{2}}. I only found out when I couldn't get the car out this morning.'
'I {{3}} think it would last this long.'
'Neither {{4}} I. It's been three weeks now, {{5}} it?'

'They {{9}}. I {{10}} nearly had an accident trying to turn around.'
'You {{11}} write to the council about it.'
'I {{12}} write – last month! They never replied.'
'Typical. Well, they {{13}} reply eventually, {{14}} they?'
'Let's hope {{15}}. In the meantime, {{16}} all just have to use the back route.'
'{{17}} – it adds ten minutes but at least it's safe.'
'Exactly. Let's spread the word to the other neighbours, {{18}} we?'

Mia and Leo have been best friends since university, and they are remarkably alike. Mia loves hiking, and {{1}} Leo. Leo has never enjoyed crowded shopping centres, and {{2}} Mia. They both agree the countryside is more peaceful, {{3}} they? Last weekend, they {{4}} try to climb the local mountain, but the weather forced them to turn back. 'I was so disappointed,' said Mia. '{{5}},' said Leo. 'But we {{6}} climb that route before without any problems, {{7}} we?' 'We {{8}},' agreed Mia. 'We {{9}} give up too easily. Let's go back next Saturday.' 'I {{10}} agree more,' said Leo. 'I've actually been looking forward to it all week.' '{{11}} have I. It'll be worth it, {{12}} it?' 'It definitely will.'

Workplace communication can make or break a team. '{{1}} you send that report to the client yet?' the manager asked. 'I {{2}} – I sent it this morning,' replied Ana. 'Good. You {{3}} have forgotten – this is critical.' 'I know it is, and I {{4}} forget. I've been following up on it all week.' Her colleague Ben agreed: '{{5}} – Ana has been really on top of it.' The manager nodded. 'I suppose you're right, {{6}} I? She {{7}} deliver when it matters.' '{{8}} does Ben,' added Ana. 'He {{9}} prepared the data pack, which I used for the report.' 'I {{10}} – it took me most of Tuesday,' Ben confirmed. 'Well, {{11}} of you have done well then,' said the manager. 'We {{12}} celebrate properly once the client signs off, {{13}} we?'

'You {{3}} still living in the same town, {{4}} you?' 'I {{5}} – I moved to the coast three years ago.' 'I {{6}} know that. Nobody told me!' 'I thought I mentioned it. I {{7}} send you a message.' 'You {{8}} – I would have remembered.' 'Well, {{9}} of us is perfect,' laughed Sara. 'Mr Harris {{10}} change at all, {{11}} he? He still walks the same way.' '{{12}} – and he still tells the same jokes!' 'He {{13}} love those jokes. He'd always look so pleased with himself.' '{{14}} he would! Happy days, {{15}} they?'

writing_prompts.vi: Viết một đoạn hội thoại giữa hai người bạn đang thảo luận về một chuyến đi sắp tới – hãy sử dụng so/neither để bày tỏ sự đồng ý, do/did để nhấn mạnh, và question tags để xác nhận thông tin
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: Auxiliary verbs: agreement (so/neither), emphasis (do/does/did), question tags
writing_prompts.example: 'I've been looking forward to this trip for months,' said Clara. 'So have I,' replied Dan. 'I haven't been abroad since before the pandemic.' 'Neither have I. It feels like forever.' 'You've packed already, haven't you?' 'I have, actually – I did start early this time, unlike last year.' 'I didn't want to mention last year.' 'Ha! Fair enough. Anyway, the hotel looks amazing, doesn't it?' 'It does. And the reviews were excellent.' 'You did check the check-in time, didn't you? I always forget that.' 'I did check – it's from three o'clock. We might need to leave our bags somewhere if we arrive earlier.' 'Good thinking. I wouldn't want to hang around for hours.' 'Neither would I. Oh, and we should book a restaurant for the first night, shouldn't we? Everywhere gets full quickly.' 'So we should. I'll look into it this evening. This is going to be wonderful, isn't it?' 'It is. I can't wait.'

writing_prompts.vi: Hãy viết về một lần bạn bị hiểu nhầm hoặc bị cho là đã làm hoặc nói điều gì đó mà bạn không làm – hãy dùng do/does/did để nhấn mạnh sự thật và question tags để kéo sự đồng tình từ người đọc
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: Auxiliary verbs: emphasis with do/did, question tags for confirmation
writing_prompts.example: A few months ago, I found myself in an awkward situation at work that I still find frustrating to think about. A colleague told our manager that I had missed an important deadline without warning. The thing is, I did send a message about it – three days before the deadline – explaining that I needed an extension because of a family emergency. I did try to follow up as well, leaving a voicemail that same afternoon. That is not the behaviour of someone who doesn't care, is it? I do care about my work – deeply – and it felt profoundly unfair to be characterised as unreliable. I did speak to my manager directly afterwards, and I showed her the original message. She did apologise, which I appreciated, but the damage to my reputation had already been done to some extent, hadn't it? The experience taught me something important: always confirm important communications in writing, and always keep records. People's memories are unreliable, aren't they? A paper trail, it turns out, is worth its weight in gold.

writing_prompts.vi: Viết về những điều bạn và người bạn thân hoặc thành viên gia đình có chung – sở thích, kinh nghiệm, cảm nhận – hãy dùng so/neither/nor để diễn đạt sự đồng cảm và tương đồng
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: Auxiliary verbs for agreement: so + aux + subject, neither/nor + aux + subject
writing_prompts.example: My sister and I are remarkably similar in many ways, despite having very different lives. She loves early mornings, and so do I – we both tend to wake up before six without an alarm. She has never been able to tolerate injustice, and neither have I; we both grew up in a household where fairness was treated as a fundamental value. She has always found it difficult to sit through long, slow films, and nor can I, which means we have been known to quietly leave the cinema early together on more than one occasion. She was nervous about starting her new job last year, and so was I on her behalf. Neither of us had expected the role to be quite so demanding so quickly. She wouldn't swap it for anything now, and neither would I if I were in her position. I sometimes think we were simply built from the same emotional blueprint, even though she ended up living in a different country and pursuing a completely different career. When we speak on the phone, it still feels like no time has passed at all.

writing_prompts.vi: Viết về một cuộc tranh luận hoặc hiểu nhầm phổ biến liên quan đến sức khỏe, khoa học hoặc xã hội – hãy dùng question tags để mời gọi sự suy ngẫm của người đọc và do/does để nhấn mạnh sự thật
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: Question tags for engagement; do/does/did for emphasis and contradiction
writing_prompts.example: There are a great many myths about sleep that most people simply accept without question, aren't there? One of the most persistent is that adults can 'catch up' on lost sleep at the weekend. It sounds reasonable, doesn't it? And yet the science does not support it – not in any meaningful, long-term way. We do know, from decades of research, that chronic sleep deprivation has serious consequences for physical health, mental clarity, and emotional regulation. Most people accept this in principle, don't they? And yet most of us do continue to sacrifice sleep in favour of work, entertainment, or social obligations. The research does show that even a single night of poor sleep impairs cognitive performance to a degree comparable with mild alcohol intoxication. You wouldn't go to work after two glasses of wine, would you? And yet the equivalent level of impairment from poor sleep is rarely treated with the same seriousness. Sleep does matter – as much as diet or exercise – and it is time we started treating it that way, isn't it? Small changes to habits can make an enormous difference. It really is that simple.

writing_prompts.vi: Hãy viết một bài phản ánh về một quyết định trong quá khứ mà người khác cho là sai, nhưng bạn tin là đúng – hãy dùng did/do để nhấn mạnh sự thật, question tags để kéo người đọc vào câu chuyện, và so/neither trong các so sánh
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: Emphasis with did/do; question tags for reader engagement; so/neither for comparison
writing_prompts.example: When I decided to leave a well-paid corporate job to start my own business at thirty-two, almost everyone around me thought I was making a serious mistake. My parents were worried, and so were most of my colleagues. I did understand their concern – I really did. The timing wasn't ideal, was it? The economy was uncertain, I had a mortgage, and I had no guarantee of income for the foreseeable future. But I did think carefully about it for over a year before making the move. I did consult a financial adviser, and I did build up savings to last me eighteen months. I do believe that preparation is everything, and neither did I rush into the decision carelessly nor did I ignore the risks. People forget that, don't they? They see the leap, but not the runway. It did work out, as it happens – not easily, and not quickly, but it worked. So did the sacrifices along the way make sense? I do stand by every one of them. The path was harder than I expected, and so was the learning curve. But I did build something I am proud of, and that is not something everyone can say, is it?
